statement.headerDoc:
STEP 987 — Happy Ending Problem f(3) = 3 (Erdős-Szekeres 1935)
  ==============================================================

  Source: Erdős & Szekeres (1935), formal-conjectures/ErdosProblems/107.lean
    (original scaffold has `theorem f_three_eq : f 3 = 3 := by sorry`)

  The function f(n) is defined as:
    f(n) = min N such that any N points in ℝ² in general position (no 3 collinear)
           contain n points forming a convex n-gon.

  Classical result:
    f(3) = 3: any 3 points in general position form a convex triangle.

  Claim: This is the DEGENERATE case of Erdős-Szekeres.
  E

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Zero-sorry verified by lake env lean
